title: A Timeskip Without Anno
wikitext:
[]: A Timeskip Without Anno             Quote Post by Merkaba » Sun Sep 07, 2014 4:33 pm I've been noticing people more and more clamoring for a series or OVA or even another film to cover Rebuild's missing 14 years, and it got me wondering: If Anno wasn't attached to the project and somebody else overtook production of such a thing, would you still watch it? Personally, I wouldn't. I don't trust Eva in the hands of anyone else. What are your thoughts?

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Sun Sep 07, 2014 4:49 pm Here's the thing about Anno not being attached to it: If Anno is done with Eva and wants to do something else, that is what khara will have to do. Animators are a precious resource and khara is still a small studio. Fighting over freelancers with other studios doesn't make for good scheduling and consistent progress. Now if Anno goes off somewhere to either shoot another live action movie or simply chill out, leaving the rest of the staff to do whatever then it might be plausible. But who's to say the rest of the staff isn't bored of Eva either after 8+ years on the same damn project? This is why I don't see an animated timeskip project as likely. Spinoff manga or novel is infinitely less of a hassle and more likely to happen. But that's also dependent on whether Anno and co. believe the entire timeskip is worth exploring beyond answering maybe 3-4 questions that matter for the main movies.

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Sun Sep 07, 2014 4:58 pm TMBounty_Hunter wrote: Spinoff manga or novel is infinitely less of a hassle and more likely to happen.

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Sun Sep 07, 2014 5:01 pm Doesn't have to be Sadamoto. I would in fact prefer for it to be anyone but him. He's good at designing stuff but an abysmal storyteller.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Sun Sep 07, 2014 5:04 pm Also, I tend to cease to care about non-Anno related Eva. If Anno’s done with Eva, then so am I.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Sun Sep 07, 2014 5:42 pm It doesn't have to be Anno, but I would want it to be somebody very closely connected to the story of the series/movies.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Sun Sep 07, 2014 5:50 pm Also even if Anno isn't the one who make it, as long as he collaborate closely so whoever do that spin-off doesn't do crap is fine with me. Seeing all the crazy awesomeness implied to have happened during the past 14 years with Asuka and especially Mari being the Wunder's main line of defense against neo-NERV or simple all the shenanigans they could had pulled off, Yamashita would be perfectly qualified for it!

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Sun Sep 07, 2014 5:53 pm Although, in this scenario, I think we're taking it to mean that Anno isn't involved with the new project in any way. That could just be me misinterpreting the OP, though.

[]:             Quote Post by Sicarius VI » Sun Sep 07, 2014 6:00 pm Now, with the story line of 14 year time skip between 2.0 and 3.0, they could definitely make a spin off show, movie, manga, etc. The story is there, they have a lot to work with in the 14 years. And with the case of it not being directed by Anno. The 14 years in between don't deal with too much of Shinji, only what he caused, therefor since Shinji isn't involved I think it would be fine. Although in honesty, unless somehow Anno ends up dying I would find it hard for him not to be involved in it. It doesn't have to be Anno, but I would want it to be somebody very closely connected to the story of the series/movies.

[]:             Quote Post by MrSniper1401 » Sun Sep 07, 2014 7:49 pm They certainly would have a lot to work with to make a spin-off show potentially work, but personally I'd rather the timeskip be addressed in Final. Namely clearing up the inconsistencies in Kaworu's infodump and showing us how Shinji got stuck with the blame for Third Impact and the landscape devastation.

[]:             Quote Post by ChaddyManPrime » Sun Sep 07, 2014 9:21 pm Does Anno have any kids?

[]:             Quote Post by Sicarius VI » Sun Sep 07, 2014 9:24 pm ChaddyManPrime wrote: Does Anno have any kids?

[]:             Quote Post by Ray » Sun Sep 07, 2014 9:27 pm As of this moment? No. Happily Married? Yes. With Children? No Not to mention Japans population is getting Older, and less and less Children are being born.

[]:             Quote Post by ChaddyManPrime » Sun Sep 07, 2014 9:28 pm I was thinking about what Miyazaki did for his son... but mostly I was thinking Child: I don't wanna get in the Director's seat. Anno: {insert name here} get in the fucking Director's seat.

[]:             Quote Post by Guy Nacks » Sun Sep 07, 2014 9:29 pm ChaddyManPrime wrote: Does Anno have any kids?

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Sun Sep 07, 2014 9:31 pm In 2002 Hideaki Anno married a fashion writer and manga artist named Moyoco. I don’t think they’ve had children, and with Moyoco in her 40’s, it seems very unlikely at this point that she can have kids. Though, if they wanted, adoption is always an option.

[]:             Quote Post by Sicarius VI » Sun Sep 07, 2014 9:32 pm Guy Nacks wrote: Anno's not going to have any kids. It's not in his character.

[]:             Quote Post by Guy Nacks » Sun Sep 07, 2014 9:35 pm Guy Nacks in 2013 wrote: From what I remember of the "Anno teaches kids" video from '98, I'm pretty sure that at some point he said that he's really uncomfortable around children, and the narrator explained that he had to be talked into doing the program. Whether he's like this now is up for debate, however, photo's of him posing with young fans are practically nonexistent, if that's any indication.

[]:             Quote Post by ChaddyManPrime » Sun Sep 07, 2014 9:38 pm He should just clone himself and teach his clone the ways of Anno.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Sun Sep 07, 2014 9:58 pm ChaddyManPrime wrote: Does Anno have any kids?

Personally, I wouldn't. I don't trust Eva in the hands of anyo
